sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Travelzoo Reports First Quarter 2026 Results
feed.summary: Travelzoo announced its financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, reporting, revenue of $24.3 million, a 5% increase year-over-year, and earnings per share of $0.23. The company saw strong renewals of memberships, which positively impacted EPS, and is investing in acquiring new Club Members. Travelzoo also repurchased 500,000 shares of its common stock during the quarter, with expectations for continued revenue growth in Q2 and subsequent quarters.

feed.title: Tranche Update on PulteGroup, Inc.'s Equity Buyback Plan announced on July 24, 2013.
feed.summary: PulteGroup, Inc. has repurchased 2,400,000 shares for $308 million between January 1, 2026, and March 31, 2026. This brings the total repurchases under their July 24, 2013, buyback plan to 203,846,863 shares, or 73.12% of the company, for $8,619.35 million. The company is actively executing its equity buyback strategy.

feed.title: Microchip Technology stock reaches all-time high at 74.11 USD
feed.summary: Microchip Technology Inc. ADR stock has hit an all-time high of $74.11, reflecting strong performance over the past year with a 71.79% total return. Despite being near its 52-week high and appearing overvalued, analysts predict the company will return to profitability this year. This growth is attributed to robust demand for semiconductor solutions and strategic business initiatives.

feed.title: Valley National Bancorp stock hits 52-week high at 13.93 USD
feed.summary: Valley National Bancorp (VLY) stock reached a 52-week high of $13.93, with shares currently trading at $14.03, reflecting a 60% surge over the past year. This performance is attributed to positive investor sentiment, strategic initiatives, and strong financial health, supported by a 3.32% dividend yield and an InvestingPro analysis indicating the stock is undervalued with a low PEG ratio of 0.28. The company also surpassed analyst expectations in its first-quarter 2026 earnings, reporting an adjusted EPS of $0.29 and revenue of $540.36 million.

feed.title: Vizsla Silver Awards Key EPCM and Mine Design Contracts for Panuco Project
feed.summary: Vizsla Silver has awarded key engineering, procurement, and construction management (EPCM) and mine design contracts to M3 Engineering & Technology Corp. and Mining Plus for its Panuco silver-gold project in Mexico. These contracts, totaling approximately US$260 million for plant and mine development, are a significant step towards de-risking the project as Vizsla advances towards potential construction, contingent on permits and financing. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates VZLA as "Neutral" due to weak financial performance and bearish technicals, despite the project's long-term potential.

feed.title: Union Pacific profit climbed 5% as it builds the case for its acquisition of rival Norfolk Southern
feed.summary: Union Pacific reported a 5% increase in first-quarter earnings, reaching $1.7 billion, despite costs related to its proposed $85 billion acquisition of Norfolk Southern. The railroad is preparing to resubmit its merger application to the U.S. Surface Transportation Board, arguing that a transcontinental railroad would enhance efficiency, lower costs, and be beneficial for customers and the country. The acquisition plan has divided opinions amongst unios and shippers, but Union Pacific CEO Jim Vena remains confident in the deal's benefits.

feed.title: ServiceNow Earnings: Impact From Acquisitions Is a Near-Term Distraction From Good Fundamentals
feed.summary: ServiceNow reported strong first-quarter revenue growth of 19% year over year, reaching $3.77 billion. The company maintained its fair value estimate of $165 per share despite some confusion surrounding the impact of recent acquisitions on performance and outlook. While acquisitions caused a near-term slowdown in backlog growth, Morningstar views the stock as attractive, emphasizing underlying fundamental strength and positive indicators like robust AI revenue and cross-sell activities.

feed.title: WD-40 Deploys AI Across Operations to Boost Efficiency and Decision-Making
feed.summary: WD-40 is integrating artificial intelligence across various operational segments to enhance efficiency and decision-making, as stated by CEO Steven Brass. The company is deploying AI-enabled platforms such as Microsoft Dynamics 365, Salesforce, and Atlas, with Dynamics 365 already active in regions accounting for about half of its global revenue. This initiative goes beyond personal productivity, aiming to redefine business processes, and WD-40 recognizes the critical need for skilled AI workers for its future success.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Univest beats Q1 2026 expectations, shares rise
feed.summary: Univest Financial Corporation reported strong Q1 2026 financial results, surpassing earnings and revenue expectations. The company posted an EPS of $0.96 against an anticipated $0.83, and revenue reached $87.5 million, exceeding the forecasted $84.33 million. Following the announcement, Univest’s stock rose by 3.38% in premarket trading, reflecting strong investor confidence and indicating potential undervaluation according to InvestingPro analysis.

feed.title: NextEra Energy (NEE) Stock Surges Past Q1 2026 Earnings Expectations
feed.summary: NextEra Energy (NEE) reported strong Q1 2026 financial results, with adjusted EPS of $1.09, significantly exceeding analyst estimates. The company's renewable energy division, NextEra Energy Resources, had its best quarter ever, adding 4 GW of new projects and expanding its development pipeline to 33 GW. NextEra Energy maintained its full-year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance and projected continued annual growth through 2032.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals stock hits 52-week high at $301.34
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals Inc. (APD) stock reached a 52-week high of $301.34, currently trading at $299.93, reflecting a strong market performance with significant year-to-date and annual gains. Despite being considered overvalued by InvestingPro analysis, the company maintains investor appeal with a 2.44% dividend yield and a 56-year history of dividend payments. Analysts have largely expressed positive sentiment, with several upgrading the stock or increasing price targets due to improved capital allocation, pricing momentum, and stable earnings growth.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Adjusts Price Target on J.M. Smucker to $104 From $118, Maintains Equalweight Rating
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has lowered its price target for J.M. Smucker (SJM) to $104 from $118, while keeping an "Equalweight" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised outlook from the financial firm. The article also lists other recent analyst adjustments and company news for J.M. Smucker.

feed.title: Tractor Supply invites shoppers to test drive mowers at Demo Days
feed.summary: Tractor Supply is holding "Demo Days" on April 25-26, inviting customers to test drive zero-turn and riding mowers at stores nationwide. Shoppers can try obstacle courses, receive a free hat, and find door-buster deals. This event is part of the larger "Yard and Garden Days" promotion, which runs through May 10 and features special offers on plants and gardening supplies.

feed.title: FirstEnergy Ohio Rate Plan Could Add $4-$5 a Month to Bills as May 22 Filing Nears
feed.summary: FirstEnergy's Ohio utilities are preparing to file a three-year rate plan by May 22 with the Public Utilities Commission of Ohio, which could increase average household bills by $4 to $5 monthly. This plan aims to secure $800 million annually for grid improvements and $83 million for tree trimming, in accordance with Ohio's new House Bill 15 rate-setting process. The proposal follows recent regulatory penalties against FirstEnergy and unresolved issues from a prior rate case, making it a critical test for the state's updated utility framework.

feed.title: Have Insiders Sold Citigroup Shares Recently?
feed.summary: Recent insider transactions at Citigroup (NYSE:C) show selling activity, including a US$3.3m share disposal by Edward Skyler and a US$7.5m sale by Ernesto Cantu within the last year. Despite the selling, insiders still own about 0.2% of the company, valued at US$505m, indicating some alignment with shareholders. No insider buying has been reported in the last year, suggesting caution despite the company's earnings growth.

feed.title: Derek Andersen has been appointed as Chief Financial Officer (CFO) at Expedia Group, Inc.
feed.summary: Expedia Group, Inc. announced the appointment of Derek Andersen as its new Chief Financial Officer, effective May 11, 2026. Andersen brings extensive financial experience from technology and consumer businesses, previously serving as CFO of Snap Inc. and holding leadership roles at Amazon. He succeeds Scott Schenkel, who will depart after the company's first-quarter earnings call.

feed.title: Analog Devices Inc Stock (ADI) Opened Up by 4.41% on Apr 23: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Analog Devices Inc. (ADI) saw its stock open up by 4.41% on April 23, driven by several positive factors. S&P Global Ratings recently upgraded ADI's credit rating to "A" with a stable outlook, reflecting the company's strong business resilience and leadership. Additionally, Analog Devices exceeded Q1 2026 revenue and EPS expectations, provided optimistic Q2 guidance, and received reiterated "Buy" ratings from analysts who cited strong demand from AI and data center markets, along with potential analog chip price increases.

feed.title: Oracle Corp Stock (ORCL) Opened Down by 4.54% on Apr 23: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Oracle (ORCL) stock opened down by 4.54% due to factors including institutional investors reducing holdings, concerns over significant AI infrastructure spending, and the recent release of a critical security patch update. Despite showing "buy" signals on some technical indicators and being rated "Buy" by analysts with an average price target of $246.61, the company faces risks from debt accumulation, execution challenges in converting performance obligations, and a class-action lawsuit.

feed.title: Results: Valmont Industries, Inc. Beat Earnings Expectations And Analysts Now Have New Forecasts
feed.summary: Valmont Industries, Inc. (NYSE:VMI) reported quarterly results exceeding analyst expectations, with revenues of US$1.0 billion and statutory earnings per share outperforming by 18%. Following these results, analysts have modestly increased their revenue and EPS forecasts for 2026, though the consensus price target remains unchanged at US$527. The company's projected revenue growth of 3.4% for 2026 is in line with its historical trend but slower than the broader industry.

feed.title: American Express CEO: Our cardholders don't care about high gas prices right now
feed.summary: The CEO of American Express stated that their cardholders are not currently concerned about high gas prices. This indicates that the company's affluent customer base may be less affected by inflationary pressures on fuel costs. The article is incomplete and does not provide further details.

feed.title: Best Buy, America’s Worst Retailer, Gets A New CEO
feed.summary: Best Buy has appointed Jason Bonfig as its new CEO, succeeding Corie Barry, whose five-year tenure saw the company's stock drop significantly and revenue decline. The article criticizes this appointment, arguing that Bonfig, as part of Barry's team, is not a suitable leader given the company's poor performance. It also highlights a recent downgrade by Goldman Sachs and faults the Board Chair, David Kenny, for the choice.

feed.title: Raymond James reiterates Strong Buy on Union Pacific stock after Q1 beat
feed.summary: Raymond James has reiterated a Strong Buy rating and a $285 price target for Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) after the company's first-quarter earnings beat estimates for adjusted operating income and EPS, despite slightly missing revenue expectations. The railroad operator reported a 3% year-over-year revenue increase to $6.22 billion and an impressive 9% rise in adjusted EPS to $2.93, reaffirming its 2026 outlook for mid-single-digit EPS growth. The company's strong operational efficiency is reflected in its 56% gross profit margin, with several other firms also maintaining positive ratings and raising price targets.

feed.title: Beyond spraying: John Deere reveals See & Scout built on precision ag tech
feed.summary: John Deere is expanding its digital farming tools with the introduction of See & Scout, a new feature built upon its existing See & Spray precision agriculture technology. See & Scout will utilize sprayer cameras to collect field data and generate insights like weed pressure maps and stand count maps, even when not actively spraying. This expansion is part of John Deere's broader digital farming strategy, which includes updates to See & Spray and integration of technologies from its Sentera acquisition.

feed.title: Leidos (LDOS) Stock Wins $617M U.S. Army Air Defense Contract
feed.summary: Leidos (LDOS) has secured a new $617 million U.S. Army contract for additional launchers for its IFPC Increment 2 air defense system, bringing its total program awards to nearly $1.2 billion. This system is crucial for defending against cruise missiles and unmanned aerial threats, reflecting increased demand due to global conflicts. The funding also supports continued research and development, with potential for further orders through 2029.

feed.title: Cognizant Technology Solutions stock hits 52-week low at $56.37 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions stock recently hit a 52-week low of $56.13, marking a significant decline of 28.78% year-to-date and 15.73% over the past year. Despite being considered undervalued by InvestingPro, the company is actively pursuing AI initiatives, including new patents and partnerships with OpenAI, Dell, and NVIDIA. Analysts have adjusted price targets, reflecting ongoing market challenges and potential AI-related deflation risks.

feed.title: Ur-Energy Inc.
feed.summary: Ur-Energy Inc. has announced the commencement of uranium mining operations at its Shirley Basin Project in Wyoming. Uranium-bearing solution is now being collected from Mine Unit 1 following successful construction, wellfield installation, and permitting. The company's shares (T.URE) are currently trading unchanged at $2.47.

feed.title: Southwest Stock Falls After Double Miss, Weak Guidance Weighs
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) stock declined after the company reported a double miss on Q1 earnings and revenue, along with weak Q2 guidance. Despite record passenger revenue in Q1 and improved results from new initiatives, the stock remains in "rebuild mode" and has struggled with overhead resistance around $43.50. The company also chose not to update full-year guidance due to macroeconomic uncertainty.

feed.title: Legacy Capital Group California Inc. Takes $1.33 Million Position in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc. $REGN
feed.summary: Legacy Capital Group California Inc. has acquired a new stake of 1,716 shares in Regeneron Pharmaceuticals, Inc., valued at approximately $1.33 million, as disclosed in its latest Form 13F filing. Major institutional investors like Dodge & Cox, Franklin Resources Inc., and Invesco Ltd. have also significantly increased their positions, raising institutional ownership to 83.31%. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Regeneron, with an average target price of $811.96, following a recent earnings beat and a dividend increase to $0.94 per share.

feed.title: FS KKR Capital Corp. $FSK is Legacy Capital Group California Inc.'s 10th Largest Position
feed.summary: Legacy Capital Group California Inc. significantly increased its stake in FS KKR Capital Corp. during the fourth quarter, making it their 10th largest holding. Despite this, several analysts have cut price targets and ratings, and the company missed its Q4 earnings and revenue estimates. An insider, Daniel Pietrzak, also recently acquired 5,000 shares, increasing his stake.

feed.title: Susquehanna Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) Stock
feed.summary: Susquehanna has increased its price target for Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) to $385 from $350, maintaining a "positive" rating and suggesting a 45.4% upside. This follows Lam Research's strong Q3 earnings, which surpassed estimates, and positive Q4 guidance, largely driven by AI-fueled chip demand. Despite significant insider selling over the past 90 days, institutional investors hold a substantial 84.6% stake in the company.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Increases Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) Price Target to $320.00
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company raised its price target for Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) to $320.00 from $290.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating. This adjustment comes after Lam Research exceeded Q3 earnings expectations and provided strong Q4 guidance, partly fueled by the high demand for AI-related chips. The new target implies a potential upside of 20.85% from the semiconductor company's current stock price.

feed.title: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) Price Target Raised to $310.00
feed.summary: Royal Bank of Canada has increased its price target for Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) to $310.00 from $290.00, reiterating an "outperform" rating after the company exceeded Q3 earnings expectations. Lam Research reported $1.47 EPS against a $1.36 consensus and $5.84 billion in revenue, prompting positive adjustments to Q4 guidance and an overall positive sentiment from analysts, although the stock trades at a high valuation. Insider selling and the company's P/E ratio over 54 suggest high expectations and limited room for error, despite strong performance and AI-driven demand.

feed.title: Snap-On Inc stock hits all-time high at 394.04 USD
feed.summary: Snap-On Inc (SNA) stock has reached an all-time high of $394.04, reflecting a strong performance with a 29% total return over the past year. Despite this, InvestingPro suggests the stock might be overvalued relative to its Fair Value. The company recently reported mixed first-quarter financial results, with earnings missing expectations but revenues surpassing forecasts, indicating both strengths and challenges in its operations.

feed.title: NextEra logs record GW-level renewables, storage origination in Q1
feed.summary: NextEra has announced a record-breaking first quarter for renewables and storage origination, securing GW-level projects. The article highlights this achievement within the broader context of the renewable energy sector in the US, indicating strong growth in solar, wind, and battery storage projects. This record suggests a significant expansion in NextEra's clean energy portfolio.

feed.title: Danaher Corporation stock hits 52-week low at $180.00
feed.summary: Danaher Corporation's stock has reached a 52-week low of $180.00, trading at $179.50, reflecting a 5.6% decline over the past year and a 19.4% drop year-to-date. Despite analyst downward revisions, InvestingPro suggests the stock is undervalued. The company recently priced €3 billion in senior notes, and analysts at Morgan Stanley, Stifel, Guggenheim, and Barclays have adjusted their price targets and ratings, indicating ongoing financial and market activities.

feed.title: Tilray Surges 10%: Rescheduling Momentum Builds as Traders Await Official Confirmation
feed.summary: Tilray Brands (TLRY) shares surged 10% on Thursday, building on a 14% gain Wednesday, due to increasing speculation that the Trump administration will reclassify marijuana as a Schedule III drug. This potential regulatory change is expected to provide federal tax advantages and enable the expansion of Tilray's medical cannabis and pharma distribution platforms, as stated by CEO Irwin Simon. While the stock's rally is significant, the move remains speculative, with prediction markets showing cautious probabilities for rescheduling by year-end 2026, and investors are watching for official confirmation to sustain the bullish momentum.

feed.title: This CSX Analyst Turns Bearish; Here Are Top 5 Downgrades For Thursday
feed.summary: Five companies, including CSX Corp, Investar Holding Corp, ASGN Inc, Avis Budget Group Inc, and TE Connectivity PLC, received downgrades from Wall Street analysts on Thursday. Notably, a Morgan Stanley analyst downgraded CSX from Equal-Weight to Underweight, while JPMorgan downgraded Avis Budget Group.

feed.title: Earnings Update: United Community Banks, Inc. (NYSE:UCB) Just Reported Its First-Quarter Results And Analysts Are Updating Their Forecasts
feed.summary: United Community Banks, Inc. (NYSE:UCB) recently announced its first-quarter results, hitting analyst revenue and earnings per share targets. Following the release, analysts have updated their forecasts for 2026, slightly adjusting EPS expectations but largely maintaining revenue projections. The consensus price target remains unchanged at US$38.17, with analysts expecting the company's growth to outpace the wider industry.

feed.title: Results: Northern Trust Corporation Exceeded Expectations And The Consensus Has Updated Its Estimates
feed.summary: Northern Trust Corporation (NASDAQ:NTRS) recently released strong quarterly results, with earnings beating analyst expectations by 16%, leading to a 7.5% increase in share price. Analysts have updated their forecasts, becoming more bullish on the company's prospects, and raising the consensus price target by 8.9%. The company is projected to grow faster than its industry, with an accelerated annualized revenue growth of 7.7% to the end of 2026.

feed.title: Buy 5 High-Flying Old Economy Stocks of 2026 With More Room to Run
feed.summary: This article identifies five "old-economy" stocks that have shown significant growth in 2026, benefiting from a broad-based market rally beyond just AI-driven sectors. These companies—Archer-Daniels-Midland Co. (ADM), Exxon Mobil Corp. (XOM), Chevron Corp. (CVX), Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp. (WAB), and Cummins Inc. (CMI)—all hold favorable Zacks Ranks and are projected to have further upside potential. The analysis highlights specific factors driving each company's strong performance and growth forecasts.

feed.title: Virginia National Bankshares reports Q1 earnings rise, dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Virginia National Bankshares Corporation reported a significant rise in net income for Q1 2026, reaching $5.3 million ($0.97 per diluted share), a 17.2% increase from the prior year, primarily due to lower cost of funds and reduced non-interest expenses. The company's board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.36 per share, indicating a sustained commitment to shareholder returns with 14 consecutive years of dividend payments. Key financial metrics like return on average assets, return on average equity, and net interest margin all improved, while the stock trades near its Fair Value according to InvestingPro analysis.

feed.title: Air Products declares $1.81 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Air Products (NYSE:APD) has announced a quarterly dividend of $1.81 per share, continuing its 56-year streak of dividend payments. The dividend, payable on August 10, 2026, represents a 2.44% yield based on the current stock price. This news follows strong Q1 2026 earnings and several analyst upgrades, reflecting a positive outlook despite the stock trading above its perceived Fair Value.

feed.title: 1972 De Tomaso Pantera With Ford Roots Gains Attention in Florida Auction
feed.summary: A 1972 De Tomaso Pantera is currently at auction in Ocala, Florida, attracting significant attention due to its unique blend of European design and American V8 power. This particular model, originally a Ford executive pool car, features a 351 cubic inch Ford Cleveland V8 and has undergone recent mechanical updates. Its enduring appeal lies in its exotic styling combined with simpler maintenance compared to its European contemporaries, making it a notable item in the collector market.

feed.title: Mizuho Securities Adjusts Digital Realty Trust Price Target to $217 From $180, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities has increased its price target for Digital Realty Trust (DLR) to $217 from $180, while reaffirming an Outperform rating. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook from the firm, with other analysts like Stifel Nicolaus, Evercore ISI, and Wells Fargo also recently raising their price targets for the data center REIT. Digital Realty Trust specializes in developing, owning, and managing data centers globally.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoRan Stock Falls After Earnings. Lower Forecasts Weigh on Shares.
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoRan stock declined after the company lowered its 2026 copper and gold forecasts due to operational disruptions at its Grasberg mine. This overshadows the company's first-quarter earnings beat, as investors focused on the long-term impact of a fatal mud rush.

feed.title: Mizuho Securities Adjusts Equinix Price Target to $1,165 From $900, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities has increased its price target for Equinix (EQIX) to $1,165 from $900, while reiterating an "Outperform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook for the data processing center operator. The article also notes other recent analyst adjustments to Equinix's price target, all indicating strong confidence in the company.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin Stock Is Down About 20% From Its High: What a $613 Fair Value Could Mean
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin stock is down about 20% from its peak, despite strong demand for its defense products and a substantial backlog. A valuation model suggests the stock could reach $613 per share by December 2028, implying a 10.4% total return or a 3.7% annualized return from its current price of $555, based on assumptions of 5% revenue growth, 10% operating margins, and a 17.9x P/E multiple. Investors are now focusing on the company's execution in converting its backlog into sustained earnings and improved margins, rather than just new contract announcements.

feed.title: General Electric Stock Is Up 47% Over the Past Year. Here’s Why the Valuation Model Still Points to $406
feed.summary: General Electric (GE) stock is projected to rise from $276 to $406 per share, implying a 46.8% total return over the next 2.7 years, based on TIKR's valuation model which assumes 11% annual revenue growth, a 21% operating margin, and a 37x P/E multiple. This forecast comes despite an initial stock dip post-Q1 2026 earnings, where GE Aerospace reported strong results but investors reacted to macroeconomic concerns like rising oil prices and geopolitical uncertainty. The model highlights GE’s strong backlog and recurring services revenue as key drivers for future growth and its premium valuation.

feed.title: Defiance Launches STXL: 2X Leveraged ETF on Seagate Technology Holdings plc
feed.summary: Defiance ETFs has launched the Defiance Daily Target 2X Long STX ETF (STXL), a single-stock leveraged ETF providing 200% daily exposure to Seagate Technology Holdings plc (NASDAQ: STX). This ETF is designed for active traders seeking magnified, short-term bullish exposure to STX, allowing them to express high-conviction tactical views. Investors should be aware that this fund carries significant risks due to its leveraged nature and is intended for knowledgeable investors who can frequently monitor their portfolios.

feed.title: Pool Corp Q1 Earnings: EPS And Sales Beat Estimates, Stock Gains On Raised Guidance
feed.summary: Pool Corp (NASDAQ:POOL) reported first-quarter diluted earnings of $1.45 per share and net sales of $1.138 billion, both beating analyst expectations. The company also raised its full-year 2026 GAAP earnings guidance to a range of $10.87 to $11.17 per share. Following the positive financial results and lifted outlook, Pool shares saw a gain of 3.65% in premarket trading.
